message:
fix(review-r5/search-core): harden ShadowMetrics latency-delta arithmetic

`ShadowMetrics` records latency deltas between legacy and v3 search by
mapping `i64` delta to `u64` via a +1_000_000 ms offset and accumulating
into `AtomicU64`. Three unsaturated casts in that pipeline could corrupt
or panic when a stuck engine produces out-of-range latencies:

  * `ShadowComparison::compute`: `as_millis() as i64 - as_millis() as i64`
    panics in debug / wraps in release if either `Duration` exceeds
    `i64::MAX` ms. Switched to `i64::try_from(...).unwrap_or(i64::MAX)` +
    `saturating_sub` so a hung engine can't brick the metric.
  * `ShadowMetrics::record`: `(delta + 1_000_000) as u64` wraps to a huge
    u64 when `delta < -1_000_000` (a >16 min legacy hang beats v3).
    Clamp delta to the offset-trick's representable range first.
  * `ShadowMetrics::snapshot`: `(latency_sum as i64 / total as i64) -
    1_000_000` wraps once the accumulator saturates past `i64::MAX`.
    Divide in u64, then `saturating_sub` the offset.

Verified: `cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-search-core` clean,
`cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-search-core --lib` → 770 pass / 0 fail.
Clippy gate blocked by 4 pre-existing lints in `mcp-agent-mail-core::toon`
(unrelated to this fix).
